title: Dypsis sambiranensis
taxonomy: Dypsis sambiranensis is classified as a species of palm tree in the family Arecaceae. It is a member of the genus Dypsis, which includes over 120 species of palms, primarily found in Madagascar. The genus Dypsis is further grouped into the subfamily Arecoideae. Within this subfamily, Dypsis sambiranensis is specifically placed in the tribe Areceae. This classification helps to categorize and understand the evolutionary relationships among palm species.

parts: Dypsis sambiranensis is a species of palm tree that has been used in traditional medicine in Madagascar.
